title: Payment Methods, Direct Debit & Late Payment Policy
text: Payment methods: You can pay your electricity bill through the following channels: (1) Online banking or bank transfer to the account number shown on your bill. (2) Direct debit — automatic monthly payment from your bank account on the due date. (3) Credit or debit card payment via the customer portal or mobile app. (4) Cash payment at any authorised payment point (banks, post offices, payment kiosks). Direct debit setup: To set up a direct debit, log in to the customer portal, go to Payment Settings, and enter your bank account IBAN. Direct debit takes effect from the next billing cycle after setup. You will receive a notification 5 days before each direct debit collection. Payment due date: Bills must be paid within 20 days of the issue date. The due date is printed on the bill. Late payment charges: If payment is not received by the due date, a late payment fee of 1.5% of the outstanding balance will be applied to the next bill. After 60 days of non-payment, the account may be referred for disconnection. Payment plans: If you are experiencing financial hardship, you may request a payment plan by contacting customer service. Payment plans allow you to spread the cost of an overdue balance over up to 12 monthly instalments with no additional interest. To qualify, you must not have defaulted on a previous payment plan.
